question.text: Unclaimed dividends and matured deposits must remain unpaid for how many years before they are transferred to the IEPF?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Section 125 of the Companies Act, 2013, all unclaimed or unpaid amounts — including dividends, matured debentures, matured deposits, application money, call money due for refund, and interest thereon — must remain unclaimed for 7 consecutive years before they are mandatorily transferred to the IEPF.
**The Trap:** The 5-year option is tempting because some older regulations used different timelines. The 3-year and 10-year options are arbitrary distractors. Remember: 7 years is the universal threshold for all types of unclaimed corporate amounts under the Companies Act, 2013.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This 7-year rule is tested across NISM Series II-A and II-B (RTA exams) and is also relevant for NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) when discussing unclaimed redemption proceeds and dividend amounts.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advise investors to encash dividend warrants promptly. Set up automated reminders for investors whose dividends remain uncashed for more than 3 years — this gives them a 4-year window to act before the IEPF transfer becomes inevitable.
